headline.vsl_adaptation: For years, doctors told you the ringing in your ears was permanent nerve damage. But Boston Medical School just proved that's not true...

lead.opening_verbatim: For years medical science was under the illusion that ringing, jangling, buzzing and all other torturous ear noises (which doctors referred to as 'Tinnitus'), were caused by irreversible damage to the otic nerve of the inner ear. But recent medical research at Harvard Medical School has proven this is simply not true.
lead.hook_mechanism: Medical establishment was wrong + Harvard proof
lead.transition_to_body: A 'Miniature Broadcast Station' is Growing In Your Ears!

mechanism.mechanism_description: Tinnitus is not caused by irreversible nerve damage but by fractured stereocilia (tiny ear hairs) that rub together creating noise like a 'broadcast station.' GABA (gamma aminobutyric acid) is a neurotransmitter that can fade away abnormal electrical impulses in the ear. The formula stimulates GABA production to 'tranquilize' and silence intrusive ear noises.

proof_elements.verbatim: recent medical research at Harvard Medical School has proven this is simply not true
Widely acclaimed by doctors at Harvard and Yale Medical Schools
reported on in the AMA's 'New England Journal Of Medicine'
Go From Noisy To Normal In Just 5 To 7 Days!

key_sections.content_verbatim: According to Boston Medical School professors, the real culprit is a 'miniature broadcast station' growing in your ears! Inside your ears nature grows hundreds of tiny hairs, (called stereocilia), that help suppress overly loud, harmful noises. Unfortunately, viruses or harsh pollutants in the air... attack these tiny hairs and fracture them... snap them in two... just like you'd fracture a bone.

When this occurs, the jagged ends of these tiny fractured hairs rub against one another and constantly generate those ringing, clicking, humming noises all day long... and often even at night preventing you from sleep. Just imagine the pandemonium going on in your ears as hundreds of these tiny fractured hairs constantly rub against one another... producing non-stop static and noise, like a micro-mini 'broadcast station' that never goes off the air.

Doctors recently discovered that in your body there exists a neuro-transmitter fluid called GABA, (gamma aminobutyric acid), that fades away abnormal, electro-impulses in the ear---such as ringing, clicking, humming, tingling, etc.

offer.urgency_mechanism: Call within the next 15 days for trial supply
